articles.title: IMD predicts in parts of AP and Telangana despite varied weather conditions - The Hans India
articles.full_text:
IMD predicts in parts of AP and Telangana despite varied weather conditions
Varied weather conditions persist across the Telugu states, with both sunny spells and rainfall occurring simultaneously. Against this backdrop, the Meteorological Department has issued a key update regarding the weather situation. The IMD stated that the monsoon is advancing and, combined with the influence of a trough, there is a likelihood of rainfall in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana. The department warned of moderate to heavy rains and the possibility of lightning strikes in several areas.
Prakhar Jain, Managing Director of the Disaster Management Authority, stated that a trough extends from East Vidarbha and Telangana down to South Coastal Andhra. Under its influence, cloudy skies and moderate rainfall are expected on Thursday. Light to moderate rains are likely in parts of the Coastal Andhra districts, as well as Chittoor and Tirupati districts, on Thursday (June 18).
Additionally, light rains accompanied by lightning are anticipated in some areas of Bapatla, Kurnool, Nandyal, Anantapur, Sri Sathya Sai, YSR Kadapa, and Annamayya districts.
According to the Hyderabad Meteorological Centre, the monsoon is spreading across Telangana and is expected to extend to more regions within the next four days; a trough currently stretches from Telangana to South Coastal Andhra. Consequently, a rain forecast has been issued for several parts of Telangana for Thursday. Thunderstorms are predicted for Mahabubnagar, Nagarkurnool, Wanaparthy, Narayanpet, and Gadwal, with wind speeds reaching 40 to 50 kmph in some areas. It stated that rains accompanied by gusty winds and thunderstorms are expected.
Heatwave Alert
Meanwhile, a two-day heatwave alert has been issued for Telangana. A warning has been issued for 10 districts, as temperatures are likely to range between 42 and 44 degrees Celsius. Noting that heatwave conditions will persist in North Telangana, authorities advised people against venturing outdoors in the afternoon. They urged the elderly and children to remain cautious, as the intense heat is expected to continue until tomorrow.
Caution Advised...
Officials advised the public not to stand under trees or near large hoardings during rains accompanied by gusty winds and thunderstorms. They warned people to be vigilant and stay as far away as possible from any power lines that might have snapped and fallen due to the wind and rain. Farmers, laborers, and herders working in fields were urged to be extra alert and move to safe locations as soon as the sky becomes overcast and thunder is heard.
